skill.name: trade-show-finder
skill.description: Find and compare trade shows, exhibitions, expos, and industry events by market, region, date, audience, and business goal.
skill.summary: Research trade show options and turn current event data into ranked recommendations.
skill.author: LensmorOfficial

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and medium patterns, but review shows they are markdown examples, relative documentation links, keyword false positives, and Chinese text entropy. The only confirmed risk is intentional web research and external URLs, including promotional Lensmor links with UTM parameters. No prompt injection, hidden code execution, credential access, or data exfiltration evidence was found.

low_findings.title: Intentional External Web Research and Links
low_findings.description: The skill tells the assistant to use web search and includes official event URLs plus Lensmor promotional links. This is expected for trade show research, but it creates normal outbound network exposure and tracking through UTM parameters. No credential collection, hidden endpoint, or exfiltration pattern was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The locations contain direct web-search instructions and visible external links. The behavior is transparent and aligned with the skill purpose, so the risk is low.

low_findings.title: Static Analyzer Documentation False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported shell execution hits are markdown fences and natural language examples, not executable code. The path traversal hits are relative README links to sibling skills. The weak cryptography and entropy alerts appear to be keyword and non-English text false positives. No runnable script, prompt injection attempt, or malicious instruction was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review confirms the flagged content is markdown documentation and templates. There is no code path that executes commands or traverses the filesystem.

content.user_title: Find Trade Shows by Industry and Region
content.value_statement: Trade show research is slow because dates, venues, and audience data are scattered across many sources. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to verify event details and create ranked recommendations.
actual_capabilities: Finds trade shows by industry, region, date range, and audience goal.
Builds comparison tables with dates, venues, exhibitor counts, visitor counts, and focus areas.
Ranks events by relevance instead of alphabetic order.
Compares named events for attending, exhibiting, or partner scouting.
Adds context such as co-located events, travel notes, and registration timing.
Labels uncertain dates or statistics as estimated or to be confirmed.
limitations: Requires web access for current dates, venues, and attendance statistics.
Cannot guarantee accuracy when official event pages are unavailable or outdated.
Does not book booths, buy tickets, or contact event organizers.
May include promotional Lensmor links in generated output.

use_cases.title: Plan an Annual Event Calendar
use_cases.description: Build a shortlist of shows for the next year with dates, locations, and priority rankings.

use_cases.title: Choose Where to Exhibit
use_cases.description: Compare major shows by buyer fit, size, geography, and likely return for a booth investment.

use_cases.title: Research New Market Entry
use_cases.description: Find regional exhibitions that attract distributors, buyers, and industry partners in a target market.

prompt_templates.title: Find Relevant Shows
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner search for one industry and region.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find trade shows for [industry] in [region] during [time range]. Include dates, venues, size, and best-fit notes.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Named Events
prompt_templates.scenario: Compare known options before choosing one.
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare [event one], [event two], and [event three] for a [company type]. Recommend the best fit for [goal].

prompt_templates.title: Build a Shortlist
prompt_templates.scenario: Create a practical event plan for a team.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a ranked shortlist of up to 10 trade shows for [industry] in [regions]. We want to [attend or exhibit] and reach [buyer profile].

prompt_templates.title: Assess Exhibiting Strategy
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning with budget and market goals.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate which trade shows we should exhibit at for [market goal]. Consider audience fit, show size, timing, travel load, and likely competitive presence.

output_examples.input: Find medical device trade shows in Europe for 2026.
output_examples.output: A ranked table of European medical device shows, followed by top picks for market entry and notes on regulatory relevance.

output_examples.input: Compare CES, MWC, and IFA for a consumer electronics startup.
output_examples.output: A side-by-side comparison of audience fit, timing, location, show scale, and which event best supports launch goals.

output_examples.input: What shows should we attend to find distributors in the Middle East?
output_examples.output: A regional event shortlist focused on distributor discovery, buyer profile, recommended next steps, and data confidence notes.

best_practices: Provide industry, target region, time range, and business goal.
Ask for source links and confidence notes when decisions involve budget.
Use the output as a shortlist, then verify final details with official organizers.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a final booth decision without sharing goals and budget.
Do not rely on old event dates without requesting current verification.
Do not treat estimated exhibitor or visitor counts as guaranteed numbers.

faq.question: Does this skill need internet access?
faq.answer: Yes. Current event dates, venues, and attendance figures should be verified with web sources.

faq.question: Can it compare shows I already know?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the event names and your goal, and it can compare audience fit and timing.

faq.question: Can it help exhibitors and attendees?
faq.answer: Yes. It adjusts recommendations for exhibiting, attending, distributor search, or competitor scouting.

faq.question: Does it book booths or tickets?
faq.answer: No. It researches and compares events, but it does not complete bookings or payments.

faq.question: How does it handle uncertain information?
faq.answer: It should label uncertain dates or statistics as estimated or to be confirmed.

faq.question: Which tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
